title: haloragis digyna
intro: Haloragis digyna, also known as water buttercup, is a small, aquatic plant native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Haloragis digyna include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, with the leaves and stems being the most commonly used parts for medicinal purposes.
